title: How to Make 3-Ingredient Stovetop Macaroni and Cheese
transcript: [0:06] Hey everyone, this is Kenji Lopez Alt from Sirius Eats in the Food Lab. And today I'm going to show you how to make macaroni and cheese on your stove top in less than 15 minutes with just three ingredients. For two servings, you're going to need 6 oz of grated cheddar cheese, 6 oz of elbow macaroni, and 6 oz of evaporated milk. You can easily scale the recipe up or down, just equal parts by weight of each. Start by adding the macaroni to a medium saucepan or skillet. Then add just enough cold water to cover it. By using less water to cook our pasta, we can concentrate its [0:37] starch, which helps emulsify the cheese into a creamy sauce. Now add a pinch of salt and bring it to a boil over high heat, stirring frequently. Don't worry, the pasta is not going to stick to the pan and it'll cook perfectly al dente even without a huge pot of water. Keep cooking and stirring until the pasta is almost cooked through and the water has nearly dried up, about 6 minutes. Some similar recipes recommend cooking the pasta in milk, but this is dangerous. The milk can scorch. Instead, I add 6 oz of evaporated milk after the water has [1:07] been absorbed. The concentrated milk proteins in the evaporated milk also help ensure a smooth sauce is formed. Once the milk has come to a boil, I add 6 oz of cheese. Now lower the heat and stir vigorously until a creamy sauce is formed. If you let it sit too long, the sauce can thicken up. But don't worry, a little splash of water will loosen it right up again. Now you tell me, which would you rather have, the stuff from the blue box or this?
